topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the open-plan office seemed to hum a particularly sharp tune that afternoon. Liam watched Sarah from across the cubicle farm, her fingers flying across the keyboard with an effortless grace that always seemed to catch his eye. He’d been meticulously tracking quarterly reports, but his focus kept drifting to her desk, a small, almost imperceptible smile tugging at the corner of his mouth whenever she laughed at something a coworker said. Then, the email landed. A salary comparison, forwarded by HR, stark and undeniable. Sarah’s compensation package was significantly higher than his own. A strange warmth bloomed in Liam’s chest, a dizzying lightness that made his hands tremble slightly as he reread the figures. He found himself staring at the screen, not at the data, but at the way the light caught the fine hairs on her arm as she leaned in to type. He needed to talk to her. He needed to know what it felt like to be near that level of success.

[story 2]

Mark felt a sudden, unwelcome tightening in his throat when he saw the performance review summary. It detailed the compensation structure for the junior team members, and the numbers didn't lie. Chloe, the new analyst, was earning substantially more than he was. A flush crept up his neck, hot and immediate. He started avoiding eye contact, focusing instead on the potted succulent on his windowsill, trying to anchor himself against the sudden surge of feeling. Every time Chloe walked by, he’d deliberately pause, letting his gaze linger a fraction too long on the sweep of her dark hair. When she offered him a brief, polite nod in the hallway, Mark managed a stiff, overly bright smile, his pulse hammering a frantic rhythm against his ribs. He felt an urgent, almost physical need to be in her orbit, to somehow bridge the gap that the spreadsheet had so clearly defined.

[story 3]

"Did you see the latest bonus structure?" David asked, his voice carefully neutral, but his eyes were wide with a peculiar intensity. He’d stumbled upon the internal pay scale document while looking for an old project file. The revelation—that a colleague, Maria, was earning a considerable premium—hit him with the force of a physical blow. A strange, electric current shot through him, making his palms slick. He spent the rest of the day replaying snippets of Maria’s interactions—the way she spoke during meetings, the confident set of her shoulders. When she approached his desk to ask a quick question, David found himself struggling to articulate a coherent response, his usual conversational skills dissolving into a breathless stammer. He just wanted to be close enough to feel that energy radiating off her.
